name: gmail-modify
description:
Modify Gmail message labels — mark read, add or remove labels. The dedup + loop-guard leg of the email-your-assistant channel: after the assistant reads and acts on a message, mark it read (remove 'UNREAD') and/or move it out of the trigger label so the same message is never processed twice.

Accepts message ids three ways: MESSAGE_IDS (array), MESSAGE_ID (single), OR chained from an upstream node that emits messages[].id (e.g. Gmail: Get Message / Gmail: List Messages), including under an __accumulated__ multi-parent merge.

DRY_RUN defaults to TRUE (house pattern): reports exactly what it WOULD change and makes NO POST until you flip it to false. Best-effort batch — a per-message failure is recorded and the batch continues; one bad id never fails the whole run. Capped at 25 messages per invocation.

Gmail label rules: system labels ('UNREAD', 'INBOX', 'STARRED', 'IMPORTANT', 'SPAM', 'TRASH') work by name. CUSTOM labels require their label ID (looks like 'Label_1234...'), NOT the display name — pass ids as given.

Requires a vault:// reference to a Google OAuth access token in AUTH_HEADER. The controller resolves + refreshes the token at dispatch time.

AUTH_HEADER.description: vault:// path to the OAuth access token wrapped in 'Bearer ' (e.g. 'Bearer vault://oauth/gmail/{user_id}/{email}/access_token'). The controller resolves the vault reference and refreshes the token if expired.

MESSAGE_IDS.description: Gmail message ids to modify. Also accepts a single MESSAGE_ID, or ids chained from upstream messages[].id. Capped at 25 per run.

MESSAGE_ID.description: Convenience: a single message id (merged with MESSAGE_IDS and any upstream ids, deduped).

ADD_LABELS.description: Label ids to ADD. System labels ('INBOX', 'STARRED', 'IMPORTANT') by name; custom labels by their 'Label_...' id.

REMOVE_LABELS.description: Label ids to REMOVE. E.g. ['UNREAD'] to mark read. System labels by name; custom labels by their 'Label_...' id.

DRY_RUN.description: When true (default), report what WOULD change and make NO POST. Set false to apply the modifications.

setup_instructions.title: Enable the Gmail API
setup_instructions.detail: In https://console.cloud.google.com, enable the Gmail API and configure OAuth consent with the 'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.modify' scope (modify is required to add/remove labels — gmail.readonly is NOT sufficient).

setup_instructions.title: Authorize the integration
setup_instructions.detail: Complete the OAuth flow via your Talos integration UI or CLI. The controller stores the access_token + refresh_token at 'oauth/gmail/{user_id}/{email}/access_token' and 'oauth/gmail/{user_id}/{email}/refresh_token', and tracks expiry in integration_credentials.

setup_instructions.title: Configure AUTH_HEADER
setup_instructions.detail: Set AUTH_HEADER to 'Bearer vault://oauth/gmail/{your_user_id}/{your_email}/access_token'. The controller resolves the vault path + refreshes the token before dispatch. If you hit 401s, call refresh_oauth_token(vault_path) to force-refresh and read the outcome.

setup_instructions.title: Look up custom label ids
setup_instructions.detail: System labels (UNREAD, INBOX, STARRED, IMPORTANT, SPAM, TRASH) work by name. For a CUSTOM label (e.g. 'Ask'), fetch its id from GET https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/labels — the modify API needs the 'Label_...' id, not the display name.

setup_instructions.title: Test with DRY_RUN first
setup_instructions.detail: Leave DRY_RUN=true and inspect the results array (each entry status='dry_run'). When the id set + label changes look right, set DRY_RUN=false to apply.
